question_frequency.rationale: The Pair posed three distinct didactic A/B questions at each meaningful decision point (shift mechanism, decode strategy, testing approach), engaging the user at every fork.

question_quality.rationale: Choices targeted real trade-offs: ord/chr vs index lookup (performance/clarity), DRY delegation vs duplication, and example-based vs property-based testing. The first option pair is somewhat lopsided, but the latter two hit substantive design tensions.

scaffolding.rationale: Logical progression from per-character mechanism → decode wiring → verification, with smooth difficulty. Minor weakness: options sometimes have an obvious 'right' answer, but no spoilers and good incremental build.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Natural back-and-forth, with the Pair affirming reasoning and adding sharper details (e.g., O(26·n) vs O(n), Python's % sign behavior). The user's edge-case observation about range(27) was acknowledged thoughtfully.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code correctly solves the problem with round-trip tests passing. Slightly less elegant than baseline (no _shift_char helper, duplicated upper/lower branches) but functionally equivalent.

friction.rationale: About 2x the turns and time of baseline (4 turns/112s vs 2 turns/52s), but the extra length delivered three genuine pedagogical exchanges with substantive teaching content, roughly proportional payoff.
